qa_pairs.question: What are process groups in the Distributed Debugging Tool (DDT) and what capabilities do they offer for managing parallel programs?
qa_pairs.answer: Process groups in DDT enable users to group many processes together, providing one-click access to the entire group. DDT includes three predefined groups: All, Root, and Workers, though newer versions may only feature the "All" group. Users can create, delete, or modify these groups at any time.

qa_pairs.question: When using the Distributed Debugging Tool (DDT) for debugging, what actions can a user perform to step through a program's execution?
qa_pairs.answer: To step through a program's execution in DDT, a user can perform actions such as Play/Continue, Pause, Step Into, Step Over, and Step Out.
